text: Reproducibility in machine learning is a major obstacle in many current research endeavors. In addition, it is hard to capitalize on past research results. The reason for this is that results are summarized in static tables and figures that only barely capture the underlying data, the code that is used to implement a method, or the detail evaluation results. As a result, data, code and detailed evaluation results are often not made available in the relevant publication. OpenML (Open Machine Learning) aims to change the way we perform machine learning research so that it becomes more in line with a network science paradigm. The main idea behind OpenML is that of creating an information space for machine learning research where datasets, workflows (flows) and research results (runs) can be made available in an automated fashion. This should take little more than clicking a few buttons in data mining tools that we are used to such as Weka, R, MOA and RapidMiner. In the OpenML framework we create designed serendipity and achieve a dynamic division of labor that in practice helps us in solving many of the hard problems that we still have, in performing large-scale meta-research studies, and in facilitating an automatic competition for new models with all other models in existence. As a byproduct, OpenML benefits also individual researchers, their students, and practitioners by providing more visibility for their work, with new impact measures, and with what we may call a working memory of a scientific field.
